zh.Stensgaard, Svanninge Sogn, Salling Herr: 丹麥斯文德波格薩林斯萬寧
zh.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn於1270年出生在丹麥斯文德波格薩林斯萬寧。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1310年之後。

zh-hans.Stensgaard, Svanninge Sogn, Salling Herr: 丹麦斯文德波格薩林斯萬寧

nameMeanings.name: Bjørn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Walfrid.

Danish and Norwegian (Bjørn); Swedish (Björn): from a personal name and nickname meaning ‘bear’ (Old Norse bjǫrn). The Norwegian name is often a shortened form of any of various habitational names from any of the places called with this word. In Swedish it is ornamental in some cases. Compare Ursin .

variants.name: Bjornstad
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Bjorn, Carsten, Erik, Fredrik, Peer.
Norwegian (Bjørnstad): habitational name from a farm named as ‘Bjørn's farm’ (see Bjorn ), of which there are many examples, especially in southeastern Norway.

variants.name: Ursin
meanings: Scandinavian (mainly Norwegian): Latinized (humanistic) form of a surname like Danish and Norwegian Bjørn (see Bjorn ) and Bjørnsen (see Bjornsen ), from Latin ursus ‘bear’.
French: variant, influenced by the Latin name Ursus, of Oursin, itself a derivative of Ours ‘bear’.

variants.name: Bjornson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Bjorn, Erik, Sig, Arni, Berger, Birgit, Lars, Ove, Selmer, Thor.
Americanized form of Swedish Björnsson or its rare variant Björnson: patronymic from the personal name Björn (see Bjorn ).
Americanized form of Norwegian Bjørnsen, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Bjornsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Steinar, Bjorn, Knut.  German Fritz, Hans.
Danish and Norwegian (Bjørnsen): patronymic from the personal name Bjørn (see Bjorn ). Compare Ursin .

nameMeanings.name: Oluf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements anu ‘ancestor’ + leifr ‘heir, descendant’. St Olaf , King of Norway ( 995–1030 ), aided the spread of Christianity in his kingdom. The name was introduced to Britain before the Norman Conquest, but modern use as a given name in the English-speaking world originated in America, where it was taken by recent Scandinavian immigrants.

en.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn was born in 1270, in Svanninge, Sallinge, Svendborg, Denmark. He had at least 1 son. He died after 1310.

fr.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn est né en 1270, à Svanninge, Sallinge, Svendborg, Danemark. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé après 1310.

de.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn wurde 1270 in Svanninge, Sallinge, Svendborg, Dänemark geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb nach 1310.

it.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn è nato il nel 1270 nel Svanninge, Sallinge, Svendborg, Danimarca. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì dopo il 1310.

ja.Stensgaard, Svanninge Sogn, Salling Herr: デンマークスベンドボルグサリンッジスヴァニンッジ
ja.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørnは1270年にデンマークスベンドボルグサリンッジスヴァニンッジで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1310年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn(은)는 1270년 덴마크 스븐드볼그 샐링 스반잉에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1310년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn nasceu em 1270, em Svanninge, Sallinge, Svendborg, Dinamarca. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu após 1310.

ru.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn родился в 1270 г. в местности сваннинг, саллинг, свендборг, Дания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер после 1310 г.

es.lifeSummary: Oluf Bjørnsøn Bjørn nació en 1270, en Svanninge, Sallinge, Svendborg, Dinamarca. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió después de 1310.
